title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Object-Lifetime Lens: Function Pointer Compatibility
summary: A plugin registry invokes callbacks after their contexts change ownership and one implementation uses an incompatible signature. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem function pointer compatibility increment in typed callback and context registry. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Hide representation behind incomplete types and constructor, observer, mutation, and destructor contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Function Pointer Compatibility · Theory Function Pointer Compatibility
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 6579. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
